name: Teriparatide
subtitle: PTH(1-34) | Bone-Building Anabolic Peptide
overview: Teriparatide is an FDA-approved anabolic bone-building agent consisting of the first 34 amino acids of parathyroid hormone. Unlike antiresorptive osteoporosis drugs that slow bone loss, teriparatide actively stimulates new bone formation. The key to its mechanism is intermittent exposure: while continuous PTH causes bone resorption, daily injections stimulate osteoblasts more than osteoclasts, resulting in net bone formation. Clinical trials show 8% spine bone density increases and 65% reduction in vertebral fractures.
mechanism: Teriparatide binds to PTH type 1 receptors (G-protein coupled receptors) on osteoblasts, osteocytes, and renal tubular cells. This activates PKA and PKC signaling pathways that promote osteoblast activity. The intermittent daily dosing creates an 'anabolic window' where bone formation exceeds resorption. Teriparatide upregulates IGF-1 and FGF2 expression, stimulates bone formation on trabecular and cortical surfaces, and increases bone mineral density through preferential osteoblast stimulation.

items.name: Postmenopausal Osteoporosis
items.description: FDA-approved for women with osteoporosis at high risk of fracture.

items.name: Male Osteoporosis
items.description: FDA-approved for men with primary or hypogonadal osteoporosis at high risk.

items.name: Glucocorticoid-Induced Osteoporosis
items.description: FDA-approved for men and women with osteoporosis from sustained corticosteroid use.

items.name: Fracture Healing
items.description: Research interest in accelerating fracture healing and bone repair.

items.name: Dental Bone Regeneration
items.description: Investigated for jawbone regeneration in dental applications.

deliveryMethods.overview: Teriparatide is administered as a once-daily subcutaneous injection, typically in the thigh or abdomen. It comes in pre-filled pen devices (Forteo) containing a 28-day supply. Treatment is typically limited to 2 years due to theoretical bone cancer risk from long-term use (seen only in rats at high doses).

steps: Allow to reach room temperature before injection
Attach new needle for each injection
Inject subcutaneously in thigh or abdomen
Discard pen after 28 days even if medication remains

interactions.notes: Antiresorptive agents often needed after teriparatide to maintain bone gains. Can be used sequentially.
Adequate calcium and vitamin D intake recommended during teriparatide therapy.

timeline.effects: Bone formation begins; spine BMD increases
5-9% spine BMD increase; fracture risk reduction
Maximum benefits achieved; transition to maintenance

rare: Osteosarcoma (theoretical - seen in rats at high doses)
contraindications: Metabolic bone diseases other than osteoporosis
monitoring: Follow with antiresorptive therapy to maintain gains

bad: Exposed to freezing or high temperatures

references.title: Teriparatide
references.keyFindings: Intermittent PTH exposure induces bone formation more than resorption; FDA-approved for osteoporosis.

references.title: The effect of teriparatide therapy on bone density in men with osteoporosis
references.keyFindings: Spine BMD increased 5.9% (20mcg) and 9.0% (40mcg); femoral neck increased 1.5-2.9%.

references.title: Teriparatide: 2.5 Years of Experience
references.journal: Journal of Bone and Mineral Research
references.keyFindings: Reduced vertebral fractures by 65% and nonvertebral fractures by 35% after 20 months.

references.title: Parathyroid Hormone (1-84) and Teriparatide in Postmenopausal Osteoporosis
references.keyFindings: Initial formation without resorption creates 'anabolic window' for rapid bone volume increase.

quickStats.frequency: Once daily at same time each day
quickStats.storage: Pen: 2-8°C refrigerated, do not freeze. Discard after 28 days
